title: Compose Specification, Services, Projects, Dependencies, and Lifecycle · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Compose Up Down State
summary: A web, worker, and database stack needs one portable declared model with predictable naming, dependencies, health gates, and teardown. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose up down state increment in validated multi-service Compose application model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict pull, build, create, recreate, start, attach, stop, down, orphan, anonymous volume, and named volume effects before lifecycle commands.
Use depends_on conditions, healthchecks, completed jobs, required dependencies, and application retry without confusing order with readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-compose-up-down-state — Predict pull, build, create, recreate, start, attach, stop, down, orphan, anonymous volume, and named volume effects before lifecycle commands.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-compose-model-lifecycle-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-compose-model-lifecycle-3
    artifact: compose.yaml
    decision: worked docker compose model lifecycle 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      image: registry.example.test/intake@sha256:verified
      depends_on:
      db:
      condition: service_healthy
      volumes:
      intake-data:
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker compose --dry-run up --build
    failure: Reject start order as readiness proof, implicit project identity, floating images, or teardown that silently deletes required volumes.
    repair: Render the merged model, pin identities, encode health dependencies, inspect the dry run, and document down versus down --volumes.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
